fault_hypothesis: On line 73, the expiration check uses strict greater-than (`now > expires_at`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= expires_at`), so entries queried at exactly their expiration time are incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals expires_at the condition should be true (entry is expired), but the strict > makes it false, causing the wrong branch to be taken.
At the boundary where now equals expires_at, the entry must be considered expired; checks that the boundary condition is correctly handled before expires_at is used in the comparison.
In the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than expires_at; if now == expires_at we should not reach this branch.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result is not None and now >= expires_at__1)

constraints.intent: The function must never return a non-None value when now is at or past the expiration timestamp.
